figures.caption: Figure 1: Two-phase transport processes and associated research topics.
Figure 2: Multiscale nature of thermal transport processes spanning molecular, interfacial, component, and system scales.
Figure 3: Representative multidimensional static and transient ”S+TD” thermal fluids datasets.
Table 2: Representative public datasets and repositories for AI-assisted twophase analysis.
Table 3: NED3 open datasets and representative S+TD classes.
Figure 4: Categories of open-source data in the NED3 ecosystem showing that open thermal-fluid resources include test section designs, thermal fluid testbeds, and processed data.
Figure 5: SeqReg workflow from loading data to preparing sequences, defining or loading models, and analyzing prediction performance.

Figure 6: Multidimensional sequential data used by SeqReq showing a data-format strategy for 1D, 2D, and 3D input types and connects SeqReg to the S+TD framework for hydrophone, AE, image, and video datasets.

Figure 7: Data curation and physics metadata for a two-phase cooling dataset. (a) Workflow from metadata extraction to data profiling, quality assessment, and standardization. (b) Example DS-X upload in which contributor metadata, search metadata, physics metadata, and processing metadata are extracted and added to the data catalog. (c) Physics-metadata representation of a boiling video using basic information, operating conditions, performance metrics, and mechanismlevel dimensionless parameters. (d) Two-dimensional projection of the high-dimensional dataset profile in heat-transfer-coefficient versus critical-heat-flux space, where DS-X is compared with existing datasets and physical-limit references.
